---
title: "Assignment 02 - Spark Analysis"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  python: python3
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Top 5 Most Posted Job Titles

```{python}
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import plotly.express as px

spark = SparkSession.builder \
    .config("spark.driver.host", "localhost") \
    .appName("JobPostingsAnalysis") \
    .getOrCreate()

df = spark.read.option("header", "true") \
    .option("inferSchema", "true") \
    .option("multiLine", "true") \
    .option("escape", "\"") \
    .csv("./data/lightcast_job_postings.csv")

df.createOrReplaceTempView("job_postings")
```

```{python}
top_titles = spark.sql("""
    SELECT TITLE_RAW, COUNT(*) AS job_count
    FROM job_postings
    GROUP BY TITLE_RAW
    ORDER BY job_count DESC
    LIMIT 5
""")

top_titles.toPandas()
```

```{python}
top_titles_pd = top_titles.toPandas()

ax = top_titles_pd.plot(kind="bar", x="TITLE_RAW", y="job_count", legend=False)
plt.title("Top 5 Most Posted Job Titles")
plt.xlabel("Job Title")
plt.ylabel("Count")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
print(df.columns)
```


```{python}
industries_df = spark.read.option("header", "true") \
    .option("inferSchema", "true") \
    .csv("./data/industries.csv")

industries_df.createOrReplaceTempView("industries")
```


# Query 1: Industry-Specific Salary Trends Grouped by Job Title

```{python}
from pyspark.sql import functions as F

# Ver columnas disponibles (debug)
print(df.columns)

# Usar SOLO columnas seguras
tech_df = df.select(
    F.col("naics_2022_6"),
    F.col("specialized_occupation")
)

# Filtrar tecnología
tech_df = tech_df.filter(F.col("naics_2022_6").cast("string") == "518210")
tech_df = tech_df.filter(F.col("specialized_occupation").isNotNull())

# Contar en lugar de salario (temporalmente)
result = (
    tech_df.groupBy("specialized_occupation")
    .count()
    .orderBy(F.col("count").desc())
)

result.toPandas()
```
